title: cistus ladanifer subsp. sulcatus
intro: Cistus ladanifer subsp. sulcatus, also known as Rockrose, is a shrubby perennial plant species belonging to the family Cistaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The medicinal properties of Cistus ladanifer subsp. sulcatus are attributed to its essential oil, which has been shown to have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects, offering potential health benefits in wound healing, skin care, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Cistus ladanifer subsp. sulcatus include infusions, decoctions, and essential oil extracts, which can be used topically or internally, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
